title: selinum wilhelmsii
intro: Selinum wilhelmsii, also known as Wild Celery or Siberian Celery, is a perennial plant belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae) that is native to Siberia, China, and Mongolia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Selinum wilhelmsii include its ability to aid digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Selinum wilhelmsii can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional herbal medicine for its supposed benefits in treating various health conditions.
